popularStory.headline: RICHARD BALDWIN BORN 1503
popularStory.description: RICHARD1 BALDWIN was born 1503 in Dundridge, Aston Clinton, Buckingham, England, and died February 1552/53 in Aston Clinton, Buckingham, England. He married ELLEN APUKE. She was born 1507 in Aston Cli …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Baldwin I於1503年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾文多福時，他的父親Robert Baldwin27歲，母親Agnes Dolte29歲。他於大約1527年與Ellen Apuke在英國巴金哈姆爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他於1552年在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾阿斯頓 克林頓被列為yeoman。他去世於1553年2月21日之前在英國巴金哈姆爾。

nameMeanings.name: Baldwin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and North German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + wine ‘friend’, which was extremely popular among the Normans and in Flanders in the early Middle Ages. It was the personal name of the Crusader who in 1100 became the first Christian king of Jerusalem, and of four more Crusader kings of Jerusalem. It was also borne by Baldwin, Count of Flanders (1172–1205), leader of the Fourth Crusade, who became first Latin Emperor of Constantinople (1204). In North America, this surname has absorbed Dutch forms such as Boudewijn.
Irish: surname adopted in Donegal by bearers of the Gaelic surname Ó Maolagáin (see Milligan ), due to association of Gaelic maol ‘bald, hairless’ with English bald.
History: A John Baldwin from Buckinghamshire, England, arrived in the US in 1638 and settled in Milford, CT.

variants.name: Milligan
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Maolagáin ‘descendant of Maoileagán’, a personal name from a double diminutive of maol meaning ‘bald’ or ‘tonsured’.

variants.name: Baldovino
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Philippines and Colombia) and Italian: from the personal name Baldovino, from ancient Germanic Baldowin (see Baldwin ).

variants.name: Bellmann
meanings: North German:
habitational name for someone from Belle in Westphalia, Bell in the Rhineland, or Bellen near Bremen.
in Saxony, from a pet form of the ancient Germanic personal name   Baldwin  .

variants.name: Bais
meanings: Dutch (mainly North Holland): patronymic from the personal name Baije, which is a derivative of an ancient Germanic name beginning with the element bern- ‘bear’, or possibly a variant of Boije, which may be a derivative of Boidin and thus a variant of Baldwin .
Italian (Friuli Venezia Giulia and Trentino-South Tyrol): nickname derived from German weiss ‘white’.

Americanized form of Croatian and Slovenian Bajs: nickname from dialect bajs ‘bass’ (the musical instrument), figuratively ‘fatty, potbelly’. The surname in the form Bais is found in Croatia, too, where it is a less common variant.

variants.name: Baudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Etienne, Adrien, Alcide, Andre, Firmin, Francoise, Jean-Yves, Laurent, Minos, Sylvain.
French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Boudoin 1 and Bowdoin .
Altered form of French   Beaudoin  , a cognate (variant) of 1 above.

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Balkin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Emanuel, Herschel.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish female personal name Beyle meaning ‘beautiful’ (related to French belle).

English (of Norman origin): perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Baldekin, Baudekin, an Anglo-Norman borrowing of Middle Dutch Baudequin, a Flemish pet form of Baldwin . However, a clear link between Middle English Baudekin and post-medieval Balkin has not been established, and the modern name may have a different source, a variant of Balcom perhaps, by migration.

variants.name: Bawden
meanings: English: from a late variant of the Norman personal name   Baldwin   or a variant of   Bowden  .

variants.name: Baldon
meanings: English: habitational name from either Marsh Baldon or Toot Baldon in Oxfordshire, from the Old English personal name Bealda + dūn ‘hill’, or a variant of Baldwin . Occasionally the name may be from Baildon in Yorkshire (named with Old English bēgel ‘bend’ + dūn ‘hill’; recorded as Baldon in the 16th century), or from Ballidon in Derbyshire (named with Old English belg ‘bag, sack’ + denu ‘valley’).
English: variant of   Boldon  .
Italian (Veneto): from an augmentative of the personal name   Baldo  .

variants.name: Beaudoin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Andre, Serge, Jacques, Gilles, Laurent, Normand, Pierre, Alcide, Lucien, Raoul.

French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements bald ‘bold, brave’ + win ‘friend’ (see Baldwin ). Compare Baudoin , Baudouin , Beaudion , Boadway , Boardway , Bodeau , Bodoh , Bodway , Boudway , and Budway .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rickert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Baldwin I was born in 1503, in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, Robert Baldwin, was 27 and his mother, Agnes Dolte, was 29. He married Ellen Apuke about 1527, in Buckinghamshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 4 daughters. In 1552, at the age of 49, his occupation is listed as yeoman in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. He died before 21 February 1553, in Buckinghamshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Baldwin I est né en 1503, à Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert Baldwin, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Agnes Dolte, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Ellen Apuke environ 1527, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 4 filles. En 1552, à l’âge de 49 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est yeoman à Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé avant 21 février 1553, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Baldwin I 1503 in Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert Baldwin 27 und seine Mutter Agnes Dolte 29. Er hat um 1527 in Buckinghamshire, England Ellen Apuke geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 4 Töchter. 1552, als er 49 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als yeoman in Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb vor 21. Februar 1553 in Buckinghamshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Baldwin I è nato il nel 1503 nel Wendover, Buckinghamshire, England, il suo padre, Robert Baldwin, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Agnes Dolte, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Ellen Apuke circa 1527, nel Buckinghamshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 4 figlie. Nel 1552, all'età di 49 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come yeoman nel Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì prima del 21 febbraio 1553, nel Buckinghamshire, England.

ko.Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, Unite: 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈
ko.Aston Clinton St Leonard, Buckinghamshir: 영국 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴 스트 리나르드
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Baldwin I(이)가 1503년 영국 벅잉함실르 웬도버에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Baldwin(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes Dolte(은)는 29세였다. 그는 대략 1527년 영국 벅잉함실르에서 Ellen Apuke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 1552년, 49세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 애스턴 클린턴에서 yeoman이었다. 그는 1553년 2월 21일 이전 영국 벅잉함실르에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Baldwin I nasceu em 1503, em Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Baldwin, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Agnes Dolte, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Ellen Apuke aproximadamente 1527, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 4 filhas. Em 1552, com 49 anos, sua profissão aparece como yeoman em Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu antes 21 de fevereiro de 1553, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, Unite: бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания
ru.Aston Clinton St Leonard, Buckinghamshir: астон клинтон ст леонард, буккингхамшир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Baldwin I родился в 1503 г. в местности вендовир, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Baldwin было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes Dolte, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Ellen Apuke около 1527 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 4 дочери. В 1552 году, в возрасте 49 лет,  он работал по профессии yeoman в местности астон клинтон, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер перед 21 февраль 1553 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Baldwin I nació en 1503, en Wendover, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Baldwin, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Agnes Dolte, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Ellen Apuke aproximadamente 1527, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 4 hijas. En 1552, a la edad de 49 años, su ocupación registrada era yeoman en Aston Clinton, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió antes de 21 de febrero de 1553, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra.
